content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
P.O. Box 807 • 428 J Street • Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 29, 1999

Stephen Miller
Bruggeman, Smith & Peckham
524 North Mountain View Avenue
San Bernardino, CA 92401-1295

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-99-215

Dear Mr. Miller:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation today, we have withdrawn your request for written advice.

The Political Reform Act (the "Act")¹ requires the Commission to provide formal written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question, or to that person's authorized representative. (Section 83114 (b).) However, as I explained in our telephone conversation, written advice will be declined where the "requestor is seeking advice relating to past conduct." (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).) Consequently, we are unable to provide assistance with respect to the issues you raise in your letter.

Per your request, I am enclosing copies of advice letters which relate to "bona fide dating relationship" issues. I hope you find these letters helpful. If you need assistance with a specific prospective situation, please contact our office for further assistance.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Jill Stecher
Staff Counsel, Legal Division

JS:tls
Enclosures:
DeRosa Advice Letter, No. I-98-284
Munoz Advice Letter, No. I-98-281
Brown Advice Letter, No. A-96-155

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor sought written advice regarding conduct under the Political Reform Act, but the specific question cannot be determined from this withdrawal letter.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC withdrew the request for written advice because the requestor was seeking guidance relating to past conduct, which falls outside the scope of matters for which formal written advice can be provided under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a withdrawal letter (W-99-215 file number). No QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present. The letter explains the basis for withdrawal and references related advice letters on 'bona fide dating relationship' issues, though the specific underlying question is not detailed in this document.

QUESTION: The requestor sought written advice regarding conduct under the Political Reform Act, but the specific question cannot be determined from this withdrawal letter.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC withdrew the request for written advice because the requestor was seeking guidance relating to past conduct, which falls outside the scope of matters for which formal written advice can be provided under Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A).
